2604.06138.title: Generating Synthetic Doctor-Patient Conversations for Long-form Audio Summarization
2604.06138.paper_summary: Long-context audio reasoning is underserved in both training data and evaluation. Existing benchmarks target short-context tasks, and the open-ended generation tasks most relevant to long-context reasoning pose well-known challenges for automatic evaluation. We propose a synthetic data generation pipeline designed to serve both as a training resource and as a controlled evaluation environment, and instantiate it for first-visit doctor-patient conversations with SOAP note generation as the task. The pipeline has three stages, persona-driven dialogue generation, multi-speaker audio synthesis with overlap/pause modeling, room acoustics, and sound events, and LLM-based reference SOAP note production, built entirely on open-weight models. We release 8,800 synthetic conversations with 1.3k hours of corresponding audio and reference notes. Evaluating current open-weight systems, we find that cascaded approaches still substantially outperform end-to-end models.
2604.06138.paper_summary_zh: 長期上下文的音頻推理在訓練數據和評估方面都受到忽視。現有的基準針對的是短期上下文任務，而與長期上下文推理最相關的開放式生成任務對自動評估提出了眾所周知的挑戰。我們提出了一個合成數據生成管道，旨在同時作為訓練資源和受控評估環境，並將其應用於首次就診的醫生-病人對話，以SOAP筆記生成為任務。該管道有三個階段：以角色為驅動的對話生成、多說話者音頻合成（包含重疊/暫停建模）、房間聲學和聲音事件，以及基於LLM的參考SOAP筆記生成，完全基於開放權重模型。我們釋放了8,800個合成對話，對應1.3k小時的音頻和參考筆記。在評估當前的開放權重系統時，我們發現級聯方法仍然顯著優於端到端模型。
2604.06138.author: Yanis Labrak et.al.
2604.06138.authors: Yanis Labrak, David Grünert, Séverin Baroudi, Jiyun Chun, Pawel Cyrta, Sergio Burdisso, Ahmed Hassoon, David Liu, Adam Rothschild, Reed Van Deusen, Petr Motlicek, Andrew Perrault, Ricard Marxer, Thomas Schaaf
